qa_pair.question: How many distinct metric names does Prometheus know about in total? Use prometheus_list_metrics without any pattern filter. Respond with an integer.
How many metric names contain the substring "http" (case-insensitive)? Use prometheus_list_metrics with pattern="http". Respond with an integer.
What is the current value of the "up" metric for the Prometheus instance itself (job="prometheus")? Use prometheus_query with query='up{job="prometheus"}'. Respond with "1" (up) or "0" (down).
How many time series are returned by the instant query 'up'? Use prometheus_query with query="up". Respond with an integer (the result_count).

For the range query 'up' over the last 1 hour with step='5m', how many series are returned? Use prometheus_query_range with appropriate start/end and step="5m". Respond with an integer (series_count).
What is the total number of active scrape targets? Use prometheus_list_targets with state="active". Respond with an integer (total_count).
How many scrape targets are currently in "down" state? Use prometheus_list_targets with state="active". Respond with an integer (down_count).
How many distinct jobs appear in the job_summary of prometheus_list_targets? Respond with an integer.
How many alerts are currently in "firing" state? Use prometheus_list_alerts. Respond with an integer (firing_count).

What is the alertname of the first firing alert (alphabetically by alertname)? Use prometheus_list_alerts and sort by alertname. If there are no firing alerts, respond with "none". Respond with the alertname string.
